methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $46M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Housing & Construction (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Housing & Construction, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Inversion Space
private.rationale: Same sector (Housing & Construction), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Material Evolution
private.rationale: Same sector (Housing & Construction), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Cobod is a Series A-stage construction technology company in the Housing & Construction sector. The target universe for comparison purposes includes three private peers—ICON, Inversion Space, and Material Evolution—though sector-specific same-stage comparable data remains sparse. No public construction companies provide sufficient methodological support for direct peer benchmarking.

Given data constraints, valuation relies on stage-progression methodology applied to Cobod's $46M in total capital raised. Using a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement yields an implied valuation of $92M. This approach treats cumulative fundraising as a floor-level proxy for company value and applies observed market step-ups to project post-series positioning. No peer-median method was applicable; the Housing & Construction sector contained only one comparable, falling below the three-datapoint threshold required for statistically meaningful cross-company valuation.

The triangulation analysis supports a valuation range of $78M (low) to $106M (high), with a median point of $92M. This single-method result carries meaningful uncertainty. Key limitations include reliance on a stage-progression step-up rather than revenue multiples or discounted cash flow, absence of financial performance data for Cobod or peers, and thinness of the Housing & Construction comp universe. Validation of this range would benefit from additional same-sector Series A+ benchmarks or disclosure of Cobod's revenue and unit economics relative to peer set participants.
